channel.title: INtech
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Cohere launches a family of open multilingual models
item.description: Cohere's Tiny Aya models support over 70 langauges

item.title: The Small English Town Swept Up in the Global AI Arms Race
item.description: The residents of Potters Bar are working to protect the “green belt” of farms, forests, and meadows that surround London from the endless demand for A...

item.title: Inside the App Where Queer Gooners Run Free
item.description: In light of Zoom crackdowns and Skype shutting down, Batemates has emerged as an alternative for “bators” who like masturbating together online.

item.title: Amazon Props Up Misleading, Junky Laptops No One Should Buy
item.description: I review laptops for a living, and it kills me when I see the PCs that Amazon and other retailers filter to the top.

item.title: After all the hype, some AI experts don’t think OpenClaw is all that exciting
item.description: "From an AI research perspective, this is nothing novel," one expert told TechCrunch.

item.title: Saatva Memory Foam Hybrid Mattress Review: Going for Gold and Good Sleep
item.description: The Saatva Memory Foam Hybrid has been chosen for Olympians. Could it be the one for you, too?

item.title: How Ricursive Intelligence raised $335M at a $4B valuation in 4 months
item.description: The reason why this nascent startup had VCs lining up is the founders.They are so famed in the AI world, everyone tried to hire them.

item.title: The El Paso No-Fly Debacle Is Just the Beginning of a Drone Defense Mess
item.description: Fears over a drug cartel drone over Texas sparked a recent airspace shutdown in El Paso and New Mexico, highlighting just how tricky it can be to depl...

item.title: Have money, will travel: a16z’s hunt for the next European unicorn
item.description: According to a16z, it has eyes around the world in order to spot companies as early as local funds might.

item.title: Flapping Airplanes on the future of AI: ‘We want to try really radically different things’
item.description: "We're exploring a different set of tradeoffs."
